q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) on line 141, a filesystem write operation that creates directories on disk — an irreversible side effect.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated; decision made at Q1.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision made at Q1.

mock_guidance.target: Mock pathlib.Path.mkdir, specifically the call target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) on line 141.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full path passed to mkdir (target.parent, derived from BASE_EXPORT_DIR / name) so the oracle can assert it does not escape BASE_EXPORT_DIR via traversal sequences like '..'.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Make the mocked mkdir a no-op that returns None, allowing execution to continue to the return statement without actually creating directories on disk.
notes: The tainted value originates from request.GET.get('name', 'packet.bin') on line 139.
Path construction is BASE_EXPORT_DIR / name on line 140, so traversal can appear in the 'name' segment.
The function also returns a JsonResponse containing str(target); the mocked mkdir should not interfere with that return path.
